channel.title: World - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Live Updates: Trump's threat to blow "everything up" if Iran won't make a deal hangs over new ceasefire bid - CBS News
item.description:
1. Live Updates: Trump's threat to blow "everything up" if Iran won't make a deal hangs over new ceasefire bid  CBS News
  2. Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges  NPR
  3. Live updates: Donald Trump holds news conference as Iran Hormuz deadline looms  The Hill
  4. Trump Says He’s Calling Iran’s Bluff in Eight-Minute Interview With WSJ  WSJ
  5. Trump warns Iran he may strike 'every power plant' as deadline to reopen Hormuz nears  Fox News

item.description:

item.title: Seoul spy agency says it’s fair to view teen daughter of North Korean leader Kim as his heir - AP News
item.description: Seoul spy agency says it’s fair to view teen daughter of North Korean leader Kim as his heir  AP NewsView Full Coverage on Google News

item.title: Exclusive: Iraq could restore oil exports to pre-war level within a week if Hormuz reopens, Basra Oil chief says - Reuters
item.description:

item.title: Iran Update Special Report, April 5, 2026 - Institute for the Study of War
item.description:
1. Iran Update Special Report, April 5, 2026  Institute for the Study of War
  2. Live: Trump says will destroy all Iran bridges, power plants in 'four hours' if no deal  France 24
  3. Airstrikes on Iran kill more than 25 as Trump's deadline to open Strait of Hormuz looms  Yahoo
  4. As Iran war enters 6th week, escalation looks the most likely scenario  The Times of Israel
  5. At least 13 killed in an airstrike southwest of Iran's capital  WCVB
item.source: Institute for the Study of War
